2606.28002.title: Dialogue to Detection: A Multimodal Hybrid NLP Pipeline for Insurance Fraud Detection
2606.28002.paper_summary: Insurance fraud imposes substantial financial losses and operational inefficiencies, raising premiums and impacting trust among legitimate policyholders. Early detection at FNOL remains a persistent challenge. Existing approaches rely largely on private, text-only datasets, limiting progress on multimodal methods that integrate linguistic, behavioural, and speaker-based indicators. We introduce a synthetic multimodal framework that replicates FNOL conditions. It generates agent-customer dialogue transcripts and two-speaker audios, performs ASR and diarisation. Downstream modules combine NER, regex-based feature extraction, LLM-RAG retrieval, and speaker embeddings in a rule-based risk score to flag narrative reuse, structural inconsistencies, and cross-case voice repetition while balancing sensitivity and false positives. Dataset validation and component-level evaluations show stability and transfer potential, offering a reproducible baseline beyond text-only fraud detection.
2606.28002.paper_summary_zh: 保險詐欺造成重大財務損失和運營效率低下，導致保費上升並影響合法保單持有者之間的信任。早期檢測在首次報案（FNOL）階段仍然是一個持續的挑戰。現有的方法主要依賴於私有的僅文本數據集，限制了在整合語言、行為和講者基礎指標的多模態方法上的進展。我們介紹了一個合成的多模態框架，模擬FNOL條件。它生成代理-客戶對話的文字記錄和雙講者音頻，執行自動語音識別（ASR）和說話者標記。下游模塊結合了命名實體識別（NER）、基於正則表達式的特徵提取、LLM-RAG檢索和說話者嵌入，使用基於規則的風險評分來標記敘述重用、結構不一致和跨案例的聲音重複，同時平衡靈敏度和假陽性。數據集驗證和組件級評估顯示出穩定性和轉移潛力，提供了一個超越僅文本詐欺檢測的可重複基準。
2606.28002.author: Muhammad Shakeel Akram et.al.
2606.28002.authors: Muhammad Shakeel Akram, Amal Htait, Abdul Hamid Sadka, Emma Meisingseth, Karishma Jaitly
